nodes.description: Chronic B-cell activation and ectopic germinal center formation in salivary glands create a microenvironment that promotes clonal B-cell expansion and progression to mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue (MALT) lymphoma. This represents the most serious complication of the disease.

evidence.reference_title: B-Cell Activation Biomarkers in Salivary Glands Are Related to Lymphomagenesis in Primary Sjögren's Disease: A Pilot Monocentric Exploratory Study.
evidence.snippet: miR-155 expression correlated with the focus score (FS), as well as BAFF-R and IL-6R expression, which were increased in primary Sjögren's disease patients and in turn related to neoplastic evolution.

evidence.reference_title: Epithelial-immune cell interplay in primary Sjögren syndrome salivary gland pathogenesis.
evidence.snippet: This continuous stimulation might ultimately drive the development of mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue lymphoma.
accompanied by a substantially increased risk to develop B-cell non-Hodgkin lymphoma, especially MALT (mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue) lymphoma

evidence.reference_title: B-Cell Activation Biomarkers in Salivary Glands Are Related to Lymphomagenesis in Primary Sjögren's Disease.
evidence.snippet: Primary Sjögren's disease is primarily driven by B-cell activation and is associated with a high risk of developing non-Hodgkin's lymphoma (NHL).
Other common manifestations include pain and fatigue, various systemic manifestations and non-Hodgkin's lymphoma.
accompanied by a substantially increased risk to develop B-cell non-Hodgkin lymphoma, especially MALT (mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue) lymphoma
